Transfer of FunctionsCivilian Conservation Corps camps transferred to Administrator of Food Production and Distribution by former section 1355(e) of the former Appendix to Title 50, War and National Defense.

Sections Continued for Purposes of Liquidation, , provided in part:Act July 12, 1943, ch. 221, title II57 Stat. 499“For the purposes hereof the provisions of the Act to establish the Civilian Conservation Corps and for other purposes approved , as amended, shall continue in full force and effect to such extent as may be necessary to facilitate liquidation of such corps, and the Federal Security Administrator shall exercise the authority vested in the Director of such corps by such Act.”June 28, 1937

Liquidation of C.C.C.Acts , title II, , and , title II, , appropriated funds to enable the Director of the Civilian Conservation Corps to provide for the liquidation of the Civilian Conservation Corps and the latter provided that “said liquidation shall be completed as quickly as possible but in any event not later than .”July 2, 1942, ch. 47556 Stat. 569July 12, 1943, ch. 22157 Stat. 499June 30, 1944Liquidation of all enrollees and overhead personnel was accomplished by , pursuant to a directive of Director of C.C.C., except for a skeleton force which was retained to transfer supplies and material to Army, Navy, and Civil Aeronautics Authority.August 15, 1942

Transfer of Equipment, , provided for the transfer during the fiscal years 1942 and 1943 of any motor or other equipment of the Civilian Conservation Corps to any department, etc. of the Government when directed by the Bureau of the Budget before .Act Dec. 23, 1941, ch. 62155 Stat. 855July 1, 1943Acts , title II, , and , title II, , authorized the disposition during the fiscal years 1943 and 1944 of camp buildings and housekeeping and camp maintenance equipment no longer needed for Civilian Conservation Corps purposes.July 2, 1942, ch. 47556 Stat. 569July 12, 1943, ch. 22157 Stat. 499

Continuance of Employment, , provided for continuance of employment without reappointment of certain employees of Emergency Conservation Work and of cooperating Federal agencies.Act July 1, 1937, ch. 425, § 150 Stat. 470

Medical and Burial ExpensesActs , title III, ; , title III, ; , title III, ; , title III, § 301, , provided for medical and burial expenses of certain enrollees of the Civilian Conservation Corps.July 12, 1943, ch. 22157 Stat. 514June 28, 1944, ch. 30258 Stat. 567July 3, 1945, ch. 26359 Stat. 377July 26, 1946, ch. 67260 Stat. 697

Transfer of FunctionsCivilian Conservation Corps and its functions consolidated with other agencies under Federal Security Agency, to be administered by Director of said Corps under direction and supervision of Federal Security Administration by Reorg. Plan No. I of 1939, §§ 201 and 207, eff. , 4 F.R. 2728, 2729, , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.July 1, 193953 Stat. 1425

Projects on Private LandsEx. Ord. No. 7677–A, § 2, , 2 F.R. 1581 DI, 1346 BV, as amended by Ex. Ord. No. 8221, , 4 F.R. 3715, authorized Director of C.C.C. to undertake projects on lands of counties and municipalities and lands in private ownership.July 1, 1937July 1, 1939
